NKJV.1: A Prayer of David. Hear a just cause, O LORD, Attend to my cry; Give ear to my prayer which is not from deceitful lips.
NKJV.2: Let my vindication come from Your presence; Let Your eyes look on the things that are upright.
NKJV.3: You have tested my heart; You have visited me in the night; You have tried me and have found nothing; I have purposed that my mouth shall not transgress.
NKJV.4: Concerning the works of men, By the word of Your lips, I have kept away from the paths of the destroyer.
NKJV.5: Uphold my steps in Your paths, That my footsteps may not slip.
NKJV.6: I have called upon You, for You will hear me, O God; Incline Your ear to me, and hear my speech.
NKJV.7: Show Your marvelous lovingkindness by Your right hand, O You who save those who trust in You From those who rise up against them.
NKJV.8: Keep me as the apple of Your eye; Hide me under the shadow of Your wings,
NKJV.9: From the wicked who oppress me, From my deadly enemies who surround me.
NKJV.10: They have closed up their fat hearts; With their mouths they speak proudly.
NKJV.11: They have now surrounded us in our steps; They have set their eyes, crouching down to the earth,
NKJV.12: As a lion is eager to tear his prey, And like a young lion lurking in secret places.
NKJV.13: Arise, O LORD, Confront him, cast him down; Deliver my life from the wicked with Your sword,
NKJV.14: With Your hand from men, O LORD, From men of the world who have their portion in this life, And whose belly You fill with Your hidden treasure. They are satisfied with children, And leave the rest of their possession for their babes.
NKJV.15: As for me, I will see Your face in righteousness;  I shall be satisfied when I awake in Your likeness.

KJV.1: A Prayer<S>8605</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Hear<S>8085</S> the right<S>6664</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, attend<S>7181</S> unto my cry<S>7440</S>, give ear<S>238</S> unto my prayer<S>8605</S>, that goeth not<S>3808</S> out of feigned<S>4820</S> lips<S>8193</S>. <sup>the right: Heb. justice</sup> <sup>not: Heb. without lips of deceit</sup>

KJV.5: Hold up<S>8551</S> my goings<S>838</S> in thy paths<S>4570</S>, that my footsteps<S>6471</S> slip<S>4131</S> not. <sup>slip: Heb. be not moved</sup>
KJV.6: I have called<S>7121</S> upon thee, for thou wilt hear<S>6030</S> me, O God<S>410</S>: incline<S>5186</S> thine ear<S>241</S> unto me, and hear<S>8085</S> my speech<S>565</S>.
KJV.7: Shew thy marvellous<S>6395</S> lovingkindness<S>2617</S>, O thou that savest<S>3467</S> by thy right hand<S>3225</S> them which put their trust<S>2620</S> in thee from those that rise up<S>6965</S> against them. <sup>by thy: or, them which trust in thee from those that rise up against thy right hand</sup>
KJV.8: Keep<S>8104</S> me as the apple<S>380</S> of the eye<S>1323</S> <S>5869</S>, hide<S>5641</S> me under the shadow<S>6738</S> of thy wings<S>3671</S>,
KJV.9: From<S>6440</S> the wicked<S>7563</S> that<S>2098</S> oppress<S>7703</S> me, from my deadly<S>5315</S> enemies<S>341</S>, who compass me about<S>5362</S>. <sup>oppress: Heb. waste</sup> <sup>my: Heb. my enemies against the soul</sup>
KJV.10: They are inclosed<S>5462</S> in their own fat<S>2459</S>: with their mouth<S>6310</S> they speak<S>1696</S> proudly<S>1348</S>.
KJV.11: They have now compassed<S>5437</S> us in our steps<S>838</S>: they have set<S>7896</S> their eyes<S>5869</S> bowing down<S>5186</S> to the earth<S>776</S>;
KJV.12: Like<S>1825</S> as a lion<S>738</S> that is greedy<S>3700</S> of his prey<S>2963</S>, and as it were a young lion<S>3715</S> lurking<S>3427</S> in secret places<S>4565</S>. <sup>Like: Heb. The likeness of him (that is, of every one of them) is as a lion that desireth to ravin</sup> <sup>lurking: Heb. sitting</sup>
KJV.13: Arise<S>6965</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, disappoint<S>6923</S> him<S>6440</S>, cast him down<S>3766</S>: deliver<S>6403</S> my soul<S>5315</S> from the wicked<S>7563</S>, which is thy sword<S>2719</S>: <sup>disappoint: Heb. prevent his face</sup> <sup>which is: or, by</sup>
KJV.14: From men<S>4962</S> which are thy hand<S>3027</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, from men<S>4962</S> of the world<S>2465</S>, which have their portion<S>2506</S> in this life<S>2416</S>, and whose belly<S>990</S> thou fillest<S>4390</S> with thy hid<S>6840</S> treasure: they are full<S>7646</S> of children<S>1121</S>, and leave<S>3240</S> the rest<S>3499</S> of their substance to their babes<S>5768</S>. <sup>which are: or, by</sup> <sup>they: or, their children are full</sup>

ESV.1: Hear a just cause, O LORD; attend to my cry! Give ear to my prayer from lips free of deceit!
ESV.2: From your presence let my vindication come! Let your eyes behold the right!
ESV.3: You have tried my heart, you have visited me by night, you have tested me, and you will find nothing; I have purposed that my mouth will not transgress.
ESV.4: With regard to the works of man, by the word of your lips I have avoided the ways of the violent.
ESV.5: My steps have held fast to your paths; my feet have not slipped.
ESV.6: I call upon you, for you will answer me, O God; incline your ear to me; hear my words.
ESV.7: Wondrously show  your steadfast love, O Savior of those who seek refuge from their adversaries at your right hand.
ESV.8: Keep me as the apple of your eye; hide me in the shadow of your wings,
ESV.9: from the wicked who do me violence, my deadly enemies who surround me.
ESV.10: They close their hearts to pity; with their mouths they speak arrogantly.
ESV.11: They have now surrounded our steps; they set their eyes to cast us to the ground.
ESV.12: He is like a lion eager to tear, as a young lion lurking in ambush.
ESV.13: Arise, O LORD! Confront him, subdue him! Deliver my soul from the wicked by your sword,
ESV.14: from men by your hand, O LORD, from men of the world whose portion is in this life.  You fill their womb with treasure;  they are satisfied with children, and they leave their abundance to their infants.
ESV.15: As for me, I shall behold your face in righteousness; when I awake, I shall be satisfied with your likeness.

NASB.1: A Prayer of David. Hear a just cause, O LORD, give heed to my cry; Give ear to my prayer, which is not from deceitful lips.
NASB.2: Let my judgment come forth from Your presence; Let Your eyes look with equity.
NASB.3: You have tried my heart; You have visited [me] by night; You have tested me and You find nothing; I have purposed that my mouth will not transgress.
NASB.4: As for the deeds of men, by the word of Your lips I have kept from the paths of the violent.
NASB.5: My steps have held fast to Your paths. My feet have not slipped.
NASB.6: I have called upon You, for You will answer me, O God; Incline Your ear to me, hear my speech.
NASB.7: Wondrously show Your lovingkindness, O Savior of those who take refuge at Your right hand From those who rise up [against them].
NASB.8: Keep me as the apple of the eye; Hide me in the shadow of Your wings
NASB.9: From the wicked who despoil me, My deadly enemies who surround me.
NASB.10: They have closed their unfeeling [heart], With their mouth they speak proudly.
NASB.11: They have now surrounded us in our steps; They set their eyes to cast [us] down to the ground.
NASB.12: He is like a lion that is eager to tear, And as a young lion lurking in hiding places.
NASB.13: Arise, O LORD, confront him, bring him low; Deliver my soul from the wicked with Your sword,
NASB.14: From men with Your hand, O LORD, From men of the world, whose portion is in [this] life, And whose belly You fill with Your treasure; They are satisfied with children, And leave their abundance to their babes.
NASB.15: As for me, I shall behold Your face in righteousness; I will be satisfied with Your likeness when I awake.

NLT.1:
O LORD, hear my plea for justice.
Listen to my cry for help.
Pay attention to my prayer,
for it comes from honest lips.
NLT.2:
Declare me innocent,
for you see those who do right.
NLT.3:
You have tested my thoughts and examined my heart in the night.
You have scrutinized me and found nothing wrong.
I am determined not to sin in what I say.
NLT.4:
I have followed your commands,
which keep me from following cruel and evil people.
NLT.5:
My steps have stayed on your path;
I have not wavered from following you.
NLT.6:
I am praying to you because I know you will answer, O God.
Bend down and listen as I pray.
NLT.7:
Show me your unfailing love in wonderful ways.
By your mighty power you rescue
those who seek refuge from their enemies.
NLT.8:
Guard me as you would guard your own eyes.
Hide me in the shadow of your wings.
NLT.9:
Protect me from wicked people who attack me,
from murderous enemies who surround me.
NLT.10:
They are without pity.
Listen to their boasting!
NLT.11:
They track me down and surround me,
watching for the chance to throw me to the ground.
NLT.12:
They are like hungry lions, eager to tear me apart —
like young lions hiding in ambush.
NLT.13:
Arise, O LORD!
Stand against them, and bring them to their knees!
Rescue me from the wicked with your sword!
NLT.14:
By the power of your hand, O LORD,
destroy those who look to this world for their reward.
But satisfy the hunger of your treasured ones.
May their children have plenty,
leaving an inheritance for their descendants.
NLT.15:
Because I am righteous, I will see you.
When I awake, I will see you face to face and be satisfied.

WEB.1: A Prayer by David. Hear, Yahweh, my righteous plea; Give ear to my prayer, that doesn’t go out of deceitful lips.
WEB.2: Let my sentence come out of your presence. Let your eyes look on equity.
WEB.3: You have proved my heart. You have visited me in the night. You have tried me, and found nothing. I have resolved that my mouth shall not disobey.
WEB.4: As for the deeds of men, by the word of your lips, I have kept myself from the ways of the violent.
WEB.5: My steps have held fast to your paths. My feet have not slipped.
WEB.6: I have called on you, for you will answer me, God. Turn your ear to me. Hear my speech.
WEB.7: Show your marvelous loving kindness, you who save those who take refuge by your right hand from their enemies.
WEB.8: Keep me as the apple of your eye. Hide me under the shadow of your wings,
WEB.9: from the wicked who oppress me, my deadly enemies, who surround me.
WEB.10: They close up their callous hearts. With their mouth they speak proudly.
WEB.11: They have now surrounded us in our steps. They set their eyes to cast us down to the earth.
WEB.12: He is like a lion that is greedy of his prey, as it were a young lion lurking in secret places.
WEB.13: Arise, Yahweh, confront him. Cast him down. Deliver my soul from the wicked by your sword;
WEB.14: from men by your hand, Yahweh, from men of the world, whose portion is in this life. You fill the belly of your cherished ones. Your sons have plenty, and they store up wealth for their children.
WEB.15: As for me, I shall see your face in righteousness. I shall be satisfied, when I awake, with seeing your form.

AMP.1:
A Prayer of David. Hear the just (righteous) cause, O LORD; listen to my loud [piercing] cry;
Listen to my prayer, that comes from guileless lips.
AMP.2:
Let my verdict of vindication come from Your presence;
May Your eyes look with equity and behold things that are just.
AMP.3:
You have tried my heart;
You have visited me in the night;
You have tested me and You find nothing [evil in me];
I intend that my mouth will not transgress.
AMP.4:
Concerning the deeds of men, by the word of Your lips
I have kept away from the paths of the violent.
AMP.5:
My steps have held closely to Your paths;
My feet have not staggered.
AMP.6:
I have called upon You, for You, O God, will answer me;
Incline Your ear to me, hear my speech.
AMP.7:
Wondrously show Your [marvelous and amazing] lovingkindness,
O Savior of those who take refuge at Your right hand
From those who rise up against them.
AMP.8:
Keep me [in Your affectionate care, protect me] as the apple of Your eye;
Hide me in the [protective] shadow of Your wings
AMP.9:
From the wicked who despoil and deal violently with me,
My deadly enemies who surround me.
AMP.10:
They have closed their unfeeling heart [to kindness and compassion];
With their mouths they speak proudly and make presumptuous claims.
AMP.11:
They track us down and have now surrounded us in our steps;
They set their eyes to force us to the ground,
AMP.12:
He is like a lion eager to tear [his prey],
And like a young lion lurking in hiding places.
AMP.13:
Arise, O LORD, confront him, cast him down;
Save my soul from the wicked with Your sword,
AMP.14:
From men with Your hand, O LORD,
From men of the world [these moths of the night] whose portion [of enjoyment] is in this life—idle and vain,
And whose belly You fill with Your treasure;
They are satisfied with children,
And they leave what they have left [of wealth] to their children.
AMP.15:
As for me, I shall see Your face in righteousness;
I will be [fully] satisfied when I awake [to find myself] seeing Your likeness.

MSG.1:
A David prayer
Listen while I build my case, God,
the most honest prayer you'll ever hear.
MSG.2:
Show the world I'm innocent—
in your heart you know I am.
MSG.3:
Go ahead, examine me from inside out,
surprise me in the middle of the night—
You'll find I'm just what I say I am.
My words don't run loose.
MSG.4:
I'm not trying to get my way
in the world's way.
I'm trying to get your way,
your Word's way.
MSG.5:
I'm staying on your trail;
I'm putting one foot
In front of the other.
I'm not giving up.
MSG.6:
I call to you, God, because I'm sure of an answer.
So—answer! bend your ear! listen sharp!
MSG.7:
Paint grace-graffiti on the fences;
take in your frightened children who
Are running from the neighborhood bullies
straight to you.
MSG.8:
Keep your eye on me;
hide me under your cool wing feathers
MSG.9:
From the wicked who are out to get me,
from mortal enemies closing in.
MSG.10:
Their hearts are hard as nails,
their mouths blast hot air.
MSG.11:
They are after me, nipping my heels,
determined to bring me down,
MSG.12:
Lions ready to rip me apart,
young lions poised to pounce.
MSG.13:
Up, God: beard them! break them!
By your sword, free me from their clutches;
MSG.14:
Barehanded, God, break these mortals,
these flat-earth people who can't think beyond today.
I'd like to see their bellies
swollen with famine food,
The weeds they've sown
harvested and baked into famine bread,
With second helpings for their children
and crusts for their babies to chew on.
MSG.15:
And me? I plan on looking
you full in the face. When I get up,
I'll see your full stature
and live heaven on earth.

NIV.1:
Psalm 17
A prayer of David.
Hear, O Lord, my righteous plea;
listen to my cry.
Give ear to my prayer—
it does not rise from deceitful lips.
NIV.2:
May my vindication come from you;
may your eyes see what is right.
NIV.3:
Though you probe my heart and examine me at night,
though you test me, you will find nothing;
I have resolved that my mouth will not sin.
NIV.4:
As for the deeds of men—
by the word of your lips
I have kept myself
from the ways of the violent.
NIV.5:
My steps have held to your paths;
my feet have not slipped.
NIV.6:
I call on you, O God, for you will answer me;
give ear to me and hear my prayer.
NIV.7:
Show the wonder of your great love,
you who save by your right hand
those who take refuge in you from their foes.
NIV.8:
Keep me as the apple of your eye;
hide me in the shadow of your wings
NIV.9:
from the wicked who assail me,
from my mortal enemies who surround me.
NIV.10:
They close up their callous hearts,
and their mouths speak with arrogance.
NIV.11:
They have tracked me down, they now surround me,
with eyes alert, to throw me to the ground.
NIV.12:
They are like a lion hungry for prey,
like a great lion crouching in cover.
NIV.13:
Rise up, O Lord, confront them, bring them down;
rescue me from the wicked by your sword.
NIV.14:
O Lord, by your hand save me from such men,
from men of this world whose reward is in this life.
You still the hunger of those you cherish;
their sons have plenty,
and they store up wealth for their children.
NIV.15:
And I—in righteousness I will see your face;
when I awake, I will be satisfied with seeing your likeness.

NRSVCE.1:
Hear a just cause, O LORD; attend to my cry;
give ear to my prayer from lips free of deceit.
NRSVCE.2:
From you let my vindication come;
let your eyes see the right.
NRSVCE.3:
If you try my heart, if you visit me by night,
if you test me, you will find no wickedness in me;
my mouth does not transgress.
NRSVCE.4:
As for what others do, by the word of your lips
I have avoided the ways of the violent.
NRSVCE.5:
My steps have held fast to your paths;
my feet have not slipped.
NRSVCE.6:
I call upon you, for you will answer me, O God;
incline your ear to me, hear my words.
NRSVCE.7:
Wondrously show your steadfast love,
O savior of those who seek refuge
from their adversaries at your right hand.
NRSVCE.8:
Guard me as the apple of the eye;
hide me in the shadow of your wings,
NRSVCE.9:
from the wicked who despoil me,
my deadly enemies who surround me.
NRSVCE.10:
They close their hearts to pity;
with their mouths they speak arrogantly.
NRSVCE.11:
They track me down; now they surround me;
they set their eyes to cast me to the ground.
NRSVCE.12:
They are like a lion eager to tear,
like a young lion lurking in ambush.
NRSVCE.13:
Rise up, O LORD, confront them, overthrow them!
By your sword deliver my life from the wicked,
NRSVCE.14:
from mortals — by your hand, O LORD —
from mortals whose portion in life is in this world.
May their bellies be filled with what you have stored up for them;
may their children have more than enough;
may they leave something over to their little ones.
NRSVCE.15:
As for me, I shall behold your face in righteousness;
when I awake I shall be satisfied, beholding your likeness.

NET.1: LORD, consider my just cause! Pay attention to my cry for help! Listen to the prayer I sincerely offer!
NET.2: Make a just decision on my behalf! Decide what is right!
NET.3: You have scrutinized my inner motives; you have examined me during the night. You have carefully evaluated me, but you find no sin. I am determined I will say nothing sinful.
NET.4: As for the actions of people — just as you have commanded, I have not followed in the footsteps of violent men.
NET.5: I carefully obey your commands; I do not deviate from them.
NET.6: I call to you for you will answer me, O God. Listen to me! Hear what I say!
NET.7: Accomplish awesome, faithful deeds, you who powerfully deliver those who look to you for protection from their enemies.
NET.8: Protect me as you would protect the pupil of your eye! Hide me in the shadow of your wings!
NET.9: Protect me from the wicked men who attack me, my enemies who crowd around me for the kill.
NET.10: They are calloused; they speak arrogantly.
NET.11: They attack me, now they surround me; they intend to throw me to the ground.
NET.12: He is like a lion that wants to tear its prey to bits, like a young lion crouching in hidden places.
NET.13: Rise up, LORD! Confront him! Knock him down! Use your sword to rescue me from the wicked man!
NET.14: LORD, use your power to deliver me from these murderers, from the murderers of this world! They enjoy prosperity; you overwhelm them with the riches they desire. They have many children, and leave their wealth to their offspring.
NET.15: As for me, because I am innocent I will see your face; when I awake you will reveal yourself to me.
